question: [File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/evm/properties/EvmProperties.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: getSemanticEvmVersion()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a crafted historical block number via the public eth_call API so that ContractCallContext.useHistorical() returns true and getRecordFile().getIndex() resolves to a block number just below a version-transition boundary in getEvmVersionForBlock(), causing the wrong EVM version (e.g., EVM_VERSION_0_30 instead of EVM_VERSION_0_34) to be selected for transaction replay, thereby producing historically inaccurate execution results that misrepresent the original transaction outcome?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/evm/properties/EvmProperties.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: getEvmVersionForBlock(long blockNumber)] Can an unprivileged external user pass a block number of exactly 44029065L (one below the mainnet EVM_VERSION_0_34 boundary) to getEvmVersionForBlock() via a historical eth_call, causing floorEntry() to return EVM_VERSION_0_30 instead of EVM_VERSION_0_34, so that a contract call that originally executed under v0.34 semantics is replayed under v0.30 semantics, reorganizing the apparent transaction history?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/evm/properties/EvmProperties.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: getEvmVersionForBlock(long blockNumber)] Can an unprivileged external user pass a negative block number (e.g., -1L) to getEvmVersionForBlock() via a historical block query, causing floorEntry() to return null and the fallback EVM_VERSION to be used instead of the historically correct version, thereby misrepresenting the EVM semantics under which a past transaction executed?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/evm/properties/EvmProperties.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: getEvmVersionForBlock(long blockNumber)] Can an unprivileged external user pass Long.MAX_VALUE as a block number to getEvmVersionForBlock(), causing floorEntry() to return the latest EVM version entry regardless of the actual historical block, so that a very old historical transaction is replayed under the newest EVM version semantics, reorganizing its apparent execution history?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/evm/properties/EvmProperties.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: getEvmVersions()] Can an unprivileged external user trigger the hierarchical fallback in getEvmVersions() by querying a TESTNET-configured node (where network.evmVersions is Collections.emptyNavigableMap()) with a historical block number, causing the DEFAULT_EVM_VERSION_MAP (mapping 0L -> EVM_VERSION) to be used instead of the correct per-network version map, so that all historical blocks are replayed under the current default EVM version rather than the historically accurate one?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/evm/properties/EvmProperties.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: getEvmVersions()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that TESTNET, PREVIEWNET, and OTHER HederaNetwork values all have Collections.emptyNavigableMap() for evmVersions, causing getEvmVersions() to always fall back to DEFAULT_EVM_VERSION_MAP for historical queries on those networks, so that historical block replays on TESTNET always use EVM_VERSION regardless of the actual block height at which the transaction occurred?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/evm/properties/EvmProperties.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: getSemanticEvmVersion()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a historical block query where ContractCallContext.getRecordFile() returns a RecordFile with index=0L, causing getEvmVersionForBlock(0L) to return EVM_VERSION_0_30 on MAINNET, so that a transaction originally executed at block 0 is replayed under v0.30 semantics even if the node's current evmVersion field is set to a newer version, creating a discrepancy in historical transaction representation?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/evm/properties/EvmProperties.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: buildTransactionProperties()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that buildTransactionProperties() sets contracts.evm.version based on the static evmVersion field (not the per-block version from getEvmVersionForBlock()), so that the VersionedConfiguration built at line 95-96 always reflects the static evmVersion rather than the historically correct per-block version, causing historical transaction replays to use incorrect EVM configuration properties?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/evm/properties/EvmProperties.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: getSemanticEvmVersion()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a historical eth_call with a block number at exactly the EVM version transition boundary (e.g., block 44029066L for MAINNET EVM_VERSION_0_34) and then submit another call at block 44029065L, and observe that the two calls return different results due to different EVM versions being selected by floorEntry(), thereby confirming the exact block at which EVM behavior changed and using this to craft misleading historical transaction proofs?
